name: The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST SP 800-145 (2011) defines cloud computing canonically: five essential characteristics (on-demand self-service, broad network access, resource pooling, rapid elasticity, measured service), three service models (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS), and four deployment models (private, community, public, hybrid). The most-cited NIST cloud publication.
relationToCloudSecurity: Foundational definitional document for cloud computing. Referenced in essentially every cloud-security framework including CCM, FedRAMP, and ISO 27017.
relationToAISecurity: Defines the deployment context within which AI systems operate; AI services map cleanly onto SaaS/PaaS/IaaS layers.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: Published 2011-09-28. Definitional document; not revised since.

current_extraction.notes: Metadata-only stub. Source PDF acquisition + extraction is TODO.
